reviews.review_text:
started this game thinking “haha deer simulator” and 20 minutes later i was crawling through a bush at 0.3 km/h because i sneezed and scared away an elk from another postcode.

graphics are so realistic i started apologising to animals before shooting them.

half the gameplay is:

walking
hearing a noise
panicking
accidentally equipping the wrong item
getting attacked by a moose built like a tank

the animals somehow hear me blink from 8 kilometres away but my character can sprint through the forest sounding like a shopping trolley falling down stairs.

10/10 game though. never knew sitting in silence staring at virtual dirt could be this stressful.

I’ve been playing this game for 7.5 years, and I can confirm that if you’re not a fan of grinding and extensive progression systems. This game will likely not be for you unless you really appreciate hunting. It is also has somewhat of an arcade feel. I wouldn’t neccesarily call it “unrealistic”. However, if you get better at the game, you can get a lot done in 1-3 hours. The introduction of Great One species which are incredibly rare, has added a lot of “grinding” for players, which can definitely draw people away from the game. This game also takes a long time to progress. It took me almost a whole year to get to the maximum level of 60. And I played for over 300 hours in that year. Across all platforms, multiple lost steam accounts, I have spent well over 7,000 hours in the game. Starting up a new save file can and will always feel slow. And you don’t get access to a lot of the weapons quickly, unless you pay extra for the DLC. Speaking of dlcs, the game is $20, there are several dlcs that add new firearms, which are free to buy in game. Most of those dlcs are around $4. The game is very reliant on paid dlcs, at least if you want to experience everything. There are 18+ reserves, the base game gives you access to just 2. And a very minimal amount of weapons. I suppose you could get away with the base game, but it’s incredibly unideal. The game is well over 100gb, and you need quite a beefy pc to run high graphics. For reference, my PC, can run Far Cry Primal on high perfectly, running this game on Low graphics, gives me about 45-50fps. So be aware of that. The game is super fun, and there’s a reason i’ve played it for so long, but it’s very unbeginner friendly. You will likely have to be very dedicated, and or appreciative of hunting as a whole to progress in this game. It’s not for everyone, however I would encourage anybody who is a fan of hunting, to try out this game. You can get a lot of content with the dlcs. I would recommend the Smokin’ Barrels Weapon pack, the AR pack, and a few maps, just to get you started. However you can play reserves you don’t own in multiplayer. But one person in the server must own the reserve dlc, or else the game will end in either 10 or 15 minutes (one of the two, i forgot which). You cannot host a multiplayer game on a reserve you do not own. 

I would highly recommend this game, and I’m sad that not many of my friends have the will to get into it anymore, but if you do, it is quite possibly the best hunting game out  there.

I've owned this game since launch and never managed to figure out what a good review for it would be. Now, due to a factor relating to the total amount of played hours, I feel obliged to.

This is a great game. This is a great game to play with friends, this is a great game to play alone, this is a great game to run on screen #1 while doing work on screen #2, and this is a great game to just chill to. I've been grinding moose for the past 300 hours, more or less ever since I picked it up again and found out there's so much new stuff since I dropped off years ago, including some really freaky-looking animals that you can only encounter if you dance naked under the moon 31 times a month including the nights when you cannot see the moon AND you do this while consuming pine needles from the trees nearby. The community science that has gone into figuring out these elusive beasts and yet no one has figured them out exactly in the past six years or so since they were released intrigues me greatly, among other things. I keep dreaming about moose, spotting the outline of one that isn't quite right, and waking up because that unnerves me so much. I figure the moment I actually find a Great One, I'm going to throw my mouse across the room and spastically turn off my PC.

All of that aside, the game in itself, beyond this freak chase, is absolutely gorgeous. The landscapes have been from the beginning breathtaking and somehow, though the game's gotten much older since I put it down, they hold up against the very best graphics of today's gaming. I was stunned when I first saw them and I continue being stunned every time I load into the game. The current variety of reserves is vast and even the ones that I own by now, which are quite a few, I have no faith in ever thoroughly exploring. It's taken me a month to scout through MOST of Revontuli Coast. I've barely even touched other maps since I started, but apparently I played a lot of Vurhonga with my friends back when, and presumably had a bunch of that scouted once upon a time. I wouldn't know - I haven't even found my favourite Cape buffalo spot yet this time around. These maps are massive, utterly gorgeous, and incredibly faithful to their locations. It is a JOY to me to find information tables scattered around Revontuli and read them in Finnish, my native language, and find that they are to the detail written as if they were real information tables in our national parks. The architecture of locations is incredible, faithful, sometimes quirky and funny but never in a way that breaks immersion - always with big personality relevant to their home regions.

The animals are also incredibly realistic, and a joy to look at. I love the function of taxidermy, so you can build your own model-viewing gallery, and with the introduction of multi-trophy poses, there's even more fun to be had with them. As an artist, I appreciate this variety of poses, and the ability to spin around the harvested animals in the trophy screen immensely, because there are very few games that provide this much material that you can perfectly apply into art as reference images. I'm collecting a veritable gallery of poses of a range of animals to draw later. I find this whole feature immensely, and wish - wink wonk - that there were a few more lodge options to use, and more animals and combinations for multi-trophy poses as well.

The realism of this game is also on its own level: it truly captures the sense of being outdoors, roaming the woods, being in nature. Even if you don't hunt, you can just go out there and enjoy a virtual hike. My hard drive is BLOATED with the number of scenic shots I've taken with the in-game camera. I immensely enjoy everything about this game, and I really do mean everything. I may have forced it upon quite a few of my friends as well at this point, and some of them have also gotten pretty hooked on it. There is a bit of a learning curve, and it can be frustrating at times, but I've never felt it's too steep to climb. You can always get something down, and that makes the journey worthwhile. And when you start getting it, you really do start getting it, and things get much easier from there.

Finally - there's an abundance of ways to play this game with no "correct" way whatsoever presented. If you like to idle in your games, like I do while I'm working or chatting with friends on my other screen, I can go into a hunting concealment in an interesting location and wait for prey to walk to me. If I want to take a hike, I can go out there and discover the map while hunting down things on foot. If I want to work towards a goal, boy does this game have some to pick from, and the elusive Great Ones are not the only achievement you can choose. I've really never played a game that truly left it entirely up to you what you want to do or how you want to do it; this is, I feel, a game with a truly open world, and full freedom of choice.

Kudos to the devs. This is a gorgeous game.

I really enjoy this game since I get to know more animals and learn something about them. The game has a chill atmosphere and the trophy hunting is fun aswell. It's a very slow game though so you gotta bring patience. Game ≠ reality. Hunting irl is weird.

The game is fun... Only if you own DLC for it. 

There are 59 DLCs that are (according to the "Content For This Game" tab under the bundles) $313 and most, if not all, should be in the base game. Sure the hunting lodges somewhat make sense as a DLC but nothing else does, the tents, ATVs, and High-Tech Hunting Pack are a must have if you want to play for longer than an hour but cost $3.99 each. The game is quite repetitive without the ATVs and tents because it takes forever to get somewhere that doesn't have a hunting cabin near by and is super hard to hunt at night without the High-Tech. The guns in the base-game are ok but aren't the best, trying to hunt large game is only possible with a Compact Bow or shotgun with the correct ammunition if you don't want to chase it around for an hour just to have it despawn.

So unless you're willing to spend more on DLC than you will on the game itself, then don't get the game at all.

This game has gotten me to buy $20.95 worth of DLC when I only spend $2.99 on the game itself (On the Steam Spring sale)
super fun game to grind with friends and yourself
Best Hunter game out there, multiplayer is cool too. My younger siblings play it a lot anyone can play this game!
I played this game for 76 hours. Liked it quite a bit. Randomly had Steamcloud corrupt my save. I checked around and this issue has persisted with this game for over 7 years.

After playing the hunter classic, I wanted to try this better graphic version from the same company. It wasn't like the classic at all. 

The hunter classic had the unique realism to it, so I went back to playing classic and deleting this version from my pc. Now the hunter classic isn't working due to some issues unknown to me (I have tried all the debugging solutions, It just won't work), and I came back to playing this as a substitue but it doesn't give me that realism I had from the classic.

Even the perceived vs written-on-map distance feels unrealistic. Like a distance of 200 meters doesn't look like that in real life! Animals habitual locations can be found and marked on map, that is a plus. But the lack of weapon options, different hunting grounds being dlcs, aiming mechanic being reliant on character development instead of weapon proficiency, and sometimes even the shots won't register because the bullet hits an invisible collider layer only because you were laying down to get a much proper aim at the animals...

And all of these problems despite being made from the same company... I am disappointed in the product I bought. Consequently, I finally decided to try other hunting games to get the same realistic experience I had from the classic.
if you like hunting this is the game for you. It has many types of animals small and large game.
Runs fine on my laptop, did not find it very engaging, but I just dont think the game was for me. Hard to tell if it's a good hunting game.

As primeira vezes eu me estressei bastante porque ficava mais caminhando do que caçando. Porém, depois de 8h investidas, posso dizer que esse jogo é bem imersivo e muito interessante. Além de bem realista, é muito bonito. Super recomendo

This game is truly something, it has very good game mechanics, it is amazing when it comes to graphics, AND it's models look as real as ever! (well, the big cats could be better, but still amazing game!) It is not just based on hunting things, for it does have storys and missions for every map you own! complete a storyline to get a legendary achievement ! It also has beautiful rare creatures known as "Great Ones" though some people will refer to them as "G1" to shorten things up. They are just as beautiful as they are rare; one may go hours or even days on end trying to hunt one. (more or less of the time trying to get it to even spawn) I have said enough, you will need to see this game for yourself to fully understand... 

Have fun!! (Watch out for the tigers!)
great game and grafics good updates and content

This game is perfect,  easy 10/10. so why do i give it a negativ review? Well its not because of any bug, or issue i have with the gameplay or graphics, its just the simple fact that you cant log in to your cotw steam account on playstation, so when i got cotw on my ps5, i wanted to log in to my pc account because i didnt want to start my progress all over, but for some reason i cant have the same apex account that i have on pc on playstaition, or at least not keep my expansions. So then i just didnt think too much of it, because i thought i could just instead buy the "Call of the wild The hunter Ultimate hunting bundle" wich is what i have for my pc, wich includes a bunch of dlc like some maps, weapons, etc. But nope, for some odd reason you cant buy that after youve bought the standard game pn playstation, wich is just stupid. I`ll change this review to positive and start playing again after youve fixed this damn problem. Thank you for reading this, i hope you can fix this.

After making the switch from P24 to pc, this game has truely reunited my love for hunting. I thought I'd be frustrated after losing all my Great Ones and diamonds, but the universal hunting expierence still resonates deeply in this game. At times it can be grindy, but slowly leveling up and getting skill points and perks points is the whole reason why I love this game.

I recommend you to play this game if you like hunting animals
it's really exciting to play this game it gives you energy
every time I come home from work I play this game and forget all the fatigue of the day at work

This game is insanely good ! But 110 Go is way too heavy. Even if you do not own all the maps you need to download them with the game. I can not play to "The Hunter" a lot beacause of the free space I need to have on my SSD.

This is a really fun open world style hunting game. I like the variety of maps and animals you can hunt, and the different ways you can choose to hunt them (ie stalking, sitting and waiting, ambush style etc). Overall 10/10 from me.

I have loved this game dearly for years, and that's why I need to tell you to stay the hell away.

This game is beautiful. This game can be amazingly fun. But it is a cash grab of DLC, minimal bug fixes, and oh my GOD it is a storage hog. Every time they drop a new DLC (which is frequent, how else are they gonna sap your cash away?) you have to download it to your game, whether you are going to buy it or not. On one hand, this allows you to check out DLC if your friend/anyone on a public server is hosting a DLC reserve. 

On the other hand. As of time of writing this review, it is 109 GB. Red Dead Redemption 2 is 118 GB. HOW IN THE NAME OF HELL IS THIS GAME ONLY 9 GB LESS THAN RDR2? This game forces you to download every damn unoptimized DLC, and its just making it bloat more and more no matter how much I do not want to buy these damn guns or feeders.

If you have a ton of storage on your PC to burn? Hell, go for it, play the game. But wait for sales and use your money wisely, because there is so much DLC here that you can drain your coffers fast if you're not careful. Saw someone else say the game is ~310$ if you're paying full price for all the current DLC.

If you're like me and don't have 109 GBs to spare to a game that just keeps bloating, do not buy this game.
can not play this game, keeps giving me error 2, which is a game save directory issue and cant find a fix for

This game is the best game ever i love hunting and this is best thisn i ever playd.
I am gona buy a loooot of money just for DLC i am never gona stop playing this game.
I love the hunter call of the willd sooo much.
Bye
Great game over 5000 hours total across pc and playstation and new content is frequently added.
yea its a good game if you want to rott in pc room or something but overall i do recomend picking this game up if you love open world games

A very beautiful game with various animal species and biomes. I
 totaly recomand!
Very chill game that helps clear the headspace and diverse hunts. Always fun to play with friends and easy enough to enjoy.
love this game with all i have i really recommend this game if you like hunting and the outdoors but cant hunt then this is the game for you
I Really enjoy this game i have spent many hours walking around seeing all it has to offer it is the best hunting game i have ever played.
its relaxing, pretty good graphics

icl its a fire game. theres lots of ways to play it, you wiill never have no goal,there will allways be moer stuff to do. you coulf be playing for 5 yoears and there will be hundreds of diferent species, furs, rarities, great ones, dimonds. what im trinna ssay is that you wont get bored of this game

The different maps and animals provide a fun experience for the player and gives them lots of options. This game was very cheap to buy but I still had a great time playing through my 117 hours of playing.

This is my idea of a vacation! Shoot as many dumb animals as you are capable of, all with no fear of legal or police punishment. I wish Real Life Maryland were like this. 
In fact, even the Black Bears will run away from you instead of attack you. It's just you, your gun, and the in-character senses to try and put bullets into flesh.
Sometimes it's annoying having to chase, or starter weapons barely stinging big game...but I guess that's all part of the process. 

The game is almost 10 years old, and it still looks beautiful, especially the scenery.

Overall, not sure what the fair price tag for it would be, but there's definitely a positive review in here somewhere.

After nearly 1000 hours spent in theHunter: Call of the Wild, I can honestly say this game is an absolute masterpiece for anyone who loves atmosphere, patience, and the satisfaction that comes from a well-earned hunt. This is not some fast-paced arcade shooter where you run around blasting everything that moves. Here, every decision matters, and success feels rewarding precisely because you have to work for it.

The greatest strength of this game is its atmosphere. The maps are unbelievably beautiful — there were countless moments when I completely forgot about hunting and simply wandered through the wilderness admiring the scenery. Sunrises over lakes, foggy mornings, distant animal calls, and the feeling of hearing a stag roar somewhere far away… the immersion is incredible. Very few games manage to create such a calming and believable world.

Another huge advantage is the realism and immersion. Tracking animals, analyzing footprints, paying attention to wind direction, choosing the right weapon and ammunition — everything makes you feel like a real hunter. And because of that, finally getting a legendary trophy feels immensely satisfying. This is not random loot dropping after a few minutes of grinding what makes the experience so rewarding.

The variety is another major reason why the game stays enjoyable even after hundreds of hours. Every reserve has its own personality, unique species, atmosphere, and hunting style. One moment you are carefully sneaking through dense forests, and the next you are observing wide open fields or hunting in snowy mountains. Even after so much time, I still found new locations, fresh strategies, and memorable moments.

The progression system and equipment are also fantastic. Starting as a beginner with basic gear and slowly becoming an experienced hunter with perfectly optimized loadouts feels genuinely rewarding. And once you begin collecting rare fur types and diamond trophies, the game becomes incredibly addictive in the best possible way.

Of course, the game can sometimes be frustrating — an animal hears you from far away, or the wind suddenly changes at the worst moment — but that challenge is exactly why successful hunts feel so satisfying. Few games reward patience and focus the way this one does.

After almost 1000 hours, I can confidently say that theHunter: Call of the Wild is one of the most relaxing, atmospheric, and rewarding games ever made. If someone is looking for a game that allows them to unwind, enjoy nature, and experience deeply satisfying progression, it is hard to find a better choice.
Good graphics, but the gameplay itself feels really slow, it would have been so much better if it was faster and it needs a pretty strong PC to run well.

The monetization of the game is pretty bad at this point, with so many new areas, and other tools that feel like they should be earnable in game, but its got a ton of relaxed fun to be had. I can only recommend picking it up on sale at this point, though.
Pretty fun, need some more experiens befor a full reconmendation
slow paced hunting game, my ancestral genes had me chasing a deer across the map for hours.

Graphics are INSANE! Game better played on a higher quality setting and or a Gaming Computer or PC
Never seen a game so attentive to detail. great job by the creators.

Fairly realistic hunting game. Even if you don't know if this game is for you, it's incredibly peaceful, the environments are beautiful.

Although the amount of DLC is a bit crazy wouldn't reccomend investing in all of that if your interested,
Very fun hunting game, always good to come back to, lots of dlcs and good times with friends.
this game is good.Good simulation of hunting but i have a ryzen 3500u and AMD Raedon graphics so i think you know how this game worked for me...

I would recommend this game to others because you get to hunt and you can put your trophies in your lodge where other people are able to see what you killed, and you can go to other maps that let you have different animals.

really realistic hunting game and I really recomend this game for the gamers who love nature and wildlife
if you are very patient and you know how to play the game right it is very fun and entertaining
I have tried so many different hunting games and they have either have terrible graphics or unrealistic hunting and then I got the hunter call of the wild and finally had something with both.
Absolutely recommend this game, just wish it was cross platform so I could play with my playstation or xbox friends. I've been playing singleplayer a lot, and only a little bit of multiplayer.
Chill, scenic, hunting. With lots of variety to hunt and cool locations to explore. It's ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ fire.
Love Hunter Call of the Wild. Once you get your first diamond trophy this gamer becomes great. So fun to play by yourself but is so much more fun if you have a friend who plays

I waited until a sale and got Silver ridge peaks, and a few other dlcs, and the game for $10. I had almost all the dlcs on my ps5 so i knew what i was doing. With the base 243. i took multiple herds of bison out. 10/10
Good for sim hunting though I would recomend to get the atv dlc  for it will make things easier
Fun game with a lot of different ways to play and beautiful views and animal designs
Great game, both single player and multiplayer allow for endless hours of enjoyment.

Love this game, some times it take alot of work in order to find that pefect kill and yes time does pass in the ral world way to quick. But I enjoy every second. All the updates are great and the game has evolved so much since day one.
One of my favorite hunting games that just keeps expanding! Beautiful graphics, and an easy hunting fix between irl hunting seasons!

Easily one of the most immersive hunting games I’ve ever played. The environments are absolutely stunning, with realistic wildlife behavior, dynamic weather, and incredible sound design that make every hunt feel authentic. Whether you’re tracking deer through dense forests or exploring huge open maps, the attention to detail is amazing.

What really stands out is the pacing — it’s relaxing, rewarding, and surprisingly intense when you finally line up the perfect shot after carefully tracking an animal for several minutes. The variety of weapons, gear, reserves, and animals keeps the gameplay fresh, and the developers continue to support the game with great updates and DLC content.

This game is perfect for players who enjoy exploration, strategy, and realism. Even if you’re not normally into hunting games, the atmosphere alone makes it worth experiencing. Highly recommended!
Never understood this as a younger player, but now? Cant wait to come home and immerse myself in the african safari while looking for that next big game <3

10/10 but herd management destroys the fun of the game and yes its optional but once you did it there is no point in returning back to normal hunt as its just way better faster and more rewarding in comparison, kinda destroyed the game fun for me but I still love it
Video quality is amazing and so is the overall gaming experience.
this game makes you walk for at least an hour per session in the direction of nowhere but hitting nice shots on cool animals makes up for it.
This hctw is very good game i love playing it i would recommend this game to anyone who love playing a hunting game

This game is actually really fun. It can be frustrating at first but if you are patient even that goes away nearly immediately. Maps are pretty and relaxing and it's just you and nature. There is a lot of content locked behind a money wall unfortunately but base game is okay enough. If you do get dlc you will have a better time and it is what it is. With that being said there is a lot of animals that are in this game and a lot to hunt. Some things could be better such as stands are in fixed locations but it isn't bad really. The maps are huge as well. All in all a fun one to try. You can even hunt with friends.

'theHunter: Call of the Wild™' is widely regarded as one of the most immersive and visually stunning hunting simulators on the market, offering a meditative experience defined by its breathtaking open-world environments. The game’s primary strength lies in its commitment to realism, requiring players to master the nuances of wind direction, noise management, and animal behavior to achieve a successful harvest. Each of the diverse reserves—ranging from the frozen taiga to the lush African savannah—is rendered with incredible attention to detail, providing a sense of scale and atmosphere that makes simply exploring the wilderness as rewarding as the hunt itself.

However, the game’s deliberate pacing and steep learning curve can be a significant barrier for players seeking immediate action. Progress is often slow, necessitating long periods of silent tracking and patience that may feel tedious to those accustomed to faster-paced shooters. Additionally, while the base game offers a substantial amount of content, the experience is heavily segmented by a vast library of paid DLC, including many popular maps and essential gear, which can make the full experience feel expensive over time. Ultimately, it is a premier simulation for those who appreciate a methodical, high-stakes tactical challenge, even if its "slow burn" gameplay and monetization model may deter more casual audiences.
It is a fun way to play with friends and enjoy the wilderness even when inside. Would recommend for people who hunt in irl.
great game all around,if you like to but your brain out of the everyday life struggles,this is the one.
super fun and the game runs good never lags at all
Very fun and high quality game. Love the possibilities of grinding for great ones. Even after all that I have the adrenaline of spawning one is still crazy. Highly recommend.
a chill hunting game with tons of dlcs the make it even more fun , also prob one of the most Realistic hunting games to date
As a hunter in real life, this game truly shows the ups and downs of hunting. The graphics are spot on, and with all of the utilities and weapons in the game, it will never get old!
Great graphics and beautiful landscape with a variety of different animals and maps to play and kill.

theHunter: Call of the Wild is the best out there in this genre. Period. Its a must play for those folks who would like to hunt with patience and fill their lodges with loads of taxidermied animals they collected as trophies. Appreciate the devs creating this master piece of a game.
(Played this game for more than 500 hours on Playstation, started over on PC now)

i think the game is pretty good/ can get a little bit boring if you are not a hunter irl but can be good. this game has a lot of depth but is very pay to win. it almost feels like you have to buy stuff to be good at it. but graphics, guns, and animals are all very well visualized.

Best Hunting Game to my opinion.
You can really relax, just explore the Maps and go on hunting advantages

The game is good, even for its age. The game mechanics can be a pain to learn, but once you do, hunting is not that difficult. Even though most of the game is just a walking simulator (but that's literally what the game is about, you're walking in woods and hunting, it's peak), it does have some nice environments. Graphics are good. 
Played the game on both Windows and Linux; not many issues that couldn't be fixed with some googling.
My main issue with the game is that there isn't a point to buying the base game alone; you need the DLCs (for ease-of-life features that should be in the base game but aren't, they could have been locked behind progression, but instead they are locked behind a pay wall), and that feels a bit cash-grabby. DLCs are reasonably priced, but given the sheer number of them, I recommend buying a bundle, waiting for a sale, or both. 
Otherwise, I would say the game is solid:
8/10
Getting an animal with rare fur, like an albino or melanistic, is quite satisfying.

Truly love this game. Its so relaxing to be in the forest, not even hunting, just experiencing the environment. I live in an area with harsh winters so being able to get this level of environmental immersion is really valuable to me.

I enjoy the slow progression of this game, its actually engaging to build my skills. I can see manyyyyyy hours of gameplay in the future!! DLC is also good and helpful, very affordable too which is bonus points! Loving this game so far :^)

Animal movement is broken as well. you look at a bird a couple of miles a way, just because you looked at it, it flies away! algorithm broken? IDK.

What else, moose are immune to sniper shots for some reason.

Devs fundamentaly misunderstand hunting. 
Ak-47s and night thermal vision to hunt the last noble buffalos and wolves of america is what people want.
Too many bugs. Lots of them have been around since the games release and still not fixed. I wont be buying from Avalanche studios again.

This game is boring. It's too realistic. Trek insanely slowly through the woods, the wind shifts direction and the animal you've been chasing for 20 minutes runs off for 10 miles without slowing down.

Plus the animals aren't even anthropomorphic? Their proportions are completely unsexy. Someone should mod this game to make it playable and enjoyable.

You have to pay for ammo, the guns you start with are awful! All you can kill early game is deer, turkeys, rabbits, and ducks! This game wants to be a realistic hunting simulator but MOST people don't want it to be as realistic as it is! Animals are to hard to take down and your rifle sight will randomly come off and the only way to put it back on is to go back to base camp which you need to FIND after 20 mins of walking! And most of the content is locked behind DLC meaning if you want the full game you have to spend over $100! Spend your money on something else!
How a game that's been out for over 9 years hasn't figured out how to make you not lose all your progress after a session is beyond me.
A wolf tried to sneak up on me and I dropped it with a .300 Magnum round. I laughed my ass off 10/10.

Bought this thinking it’d be a casual shooter, and suddenly I’m spending two real-life hours army-crawling through a bush so I don't spook an elk in a different time zone.

The pacing is incredibly slow, but in the best way possible. It’s basically a gorgeous, therapeutic hiking simulator that occasionally turns into a high-stakes stealth mission. You will ruin a perfect stalk by stepping on a single dry twig. You will track a blood trail for two miles only to realize you completely missed the vitals. But when you finally hold your breath and line up that perfect, clean shot? The dopamine hit is unreal.  
Steam

If you want fast-paced arcade action, look elsewhere. But if you want to put on a podcast, grab a coffee, and get completely absorbed in the woods for an evening, it's perfect. 10/10, would apologize to a virtual deer before shooting it again.
fun, engaging, and (from what I've seen) quite realistic! afew things that feel abit 'arcadey', but it's obviously not meant to be fully realistic. would recommend, 9.5/10
